30.reason: The '-' character is stored in str2, but l is not incremented, so the next character will overwrite it. Also, the '-' should be output as-is when no expansion occurs, but the code fails to increment l, causing the '-' to be lost.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The '-' character is stored in str2, but l is not incremented, so the next character will overwrite it. Also, the '-' should be output as-is when no expansion occurs, but the code fails to increment l, causing the '-' to be lost.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When expansion conditions are not met (e.g., different character types), the '-' should be copied to str2, but l is not incremented, causing the next character to overwrite this position. Additionally, the '-' is not output in the final result because the loop ends without handling it properly.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing l++ after placing '-' that is not expanded. This causes the next character (or the final character) to overwrite this position.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing l++ after storing the hyphen; causes overwrite later.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The code fails to increment 'l' after adding the '-' character, causing subsequent characters to overwrite it. It should be str2[l++] = str1[i]; or l++ added on the next line.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The code fails to increment 'l' after adding the '-' character, causing subsequent characters to overwrite it. It should be str2[l++] = str1[i]; or add l++ on the next line.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When expansion condition fails, '-' should be appended, but the next character (str1[i+1]) is not yet processed and will be skipped later because loop only goes to len-2]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When expansion conditions are not met, the '-' should be appended to output, but the following character after '-' is not handled correctly in non-expansion cases because the loop skips it. Also, l is not incremented here, causing missing '-' in output or overwriting.]
